 CStreamThis class represents a YAML stream potentially containing multiple documents
 CNodeAbstract base class for all Nodes
 CNullNodeA null value
 CScalarNodeA scalar node is an opaque datum that can be presented as a series of zero or more Unicode scalar values
 CBlockScalarNodeA block scalar node is an opaque datum that can be presented as a series of zero or more Unicode scalar values
 CKeyValueNodeA key and value pair
 Cbasic_collection_iteratorThis is an iterator abstraction over YAML collections shared by both sequences and maps
 CMappingNodeRepresents a YAML map created from either a block map for a flow map
 CSequenceNodeRepresents a YAML sequence created from either a block sequence for a flow sequence
 CAliasNodeRepresents an alias to a Node with an anchor
 CDocumentA YAML Stream is a sequence of Documents
 Cdocument_iteratorIterator abstraction for Documents over a Stream
 CEmptyContext
 CMappingContextTraitsThis class is similar to MappingTraits<T> but allows you to pass in additional context for each map operation
 CScalarEnumerationTraitsThis class should be specialized by any integral type that converts to/from a YAML scalar where there is a one-to-one mapping between in-memory values and a string in YAML
 CScalarBitSetTraitsThis class should be specialized by any integer type that is a union of bit values and the YAML representation is a flow sequence of strings
 CScalarTraitsThis class should be specialized by type that requires custom conversion to/from a yaml scalar
 CBlockScalarTraitsThis class should be specialized by type that requires custom conversion to/from a YAML literal block scalar
 CTaggedScalarTraitsThis class should be specialized by type that requires custom conversion to/from a YAML scalar with optional tags
 CSequenceTraitsThis class should be specialized by any type that needs to be converted to/from a YAML sequence
 CSequenceElementTraitsThis class should be specialized by any type for which vectors of that type need to be converted to/from a YAML sequence
 CDocumentListTraitsThis class should be specialized by any type that needs to be converted to/from a list of YAML documents
 CPolymorphicTraitsThis class should be specialized by any type that can be represented as a scalar, map, or sequence, decided dynamically

 CDefaultContextualFoldingSetTraitDefaultContextualFoldingSetTrait - Like DefaultFoldingSetTrait, but for ContextualFoldingSets
 CContextualFoldingSetTraitContextualFoldingSetTrait - Like FoldingSetTrait, but for ContextualFoldingSets
 CFoldingSetNodeIDRefFoldingSetNodeIDRef - This class describes a reference to an interned FoldingSetNodeID, which can be a useful to store node id data rather than using plain FoldingSetNodeIDs, since the 32-element SmallVector is often much larger than necessary, and the possibility of heap allocation means it requires a non-trivial destructor call
 CFoldingSetNodeIDFoldingSetNodeID - This class is used to gather all the unique data bits of a node

 CFoldingSetImplFoldingSetImpl - An implementation detail that lets us share code between FoldingSet and ContextualFoldingSet
 CFoldingSetFoldingSet - This template class is used to instantiate a specialized implementation of the folding set to the node class T
 CContextualFoldingSetContextualFoldingSet - This template class is a further refinement of FoldingSet which provides a context argument when calling Profile on its nodes
 CFoldingSetVectorFoldingSetVector - This template class combines a FoldingSet and a vector to provide the interface of FoldingSet but with deterministic iteration order based on the insertion order
 CFoldingSetIteratorImplFoldingSetIteratorImpl - This is the common iterator support shared by all folding sets, which knows how to walk the folding set hash table
 CFoldingSetBucketIteratorImplFoldingSetBucketIteratorImpl - This is the common bucket iterator support shared by all folding sets, which knows how to walk a particular bucket of a folding set hash table
 CFoldingSetNodeWrapperFoldingSetNodeWrapper - This template class is used to "wrap" arbitrary types in an enclosing object so that they can be inserted into FoldingSets
 CFastFoldingSetNodeFastFoldingSetNode - This is a subclass of FoldingSetNode which stores a FoldingSetNodeID value rather than requiring the node to recompute it each time it is needed

 CFixedNumOperandTraitsFixedNumOperandTraits - determine the allocation regime of the Use array when it is a prefix to the User object, and the number of Use objects is known at compile time
 COptionalOperandTraitsOptionalOperandTraits - when the number of operands may change at runtime
 CVariadicOperandTraitsVariadicOperandTraits - determine the allocation regime of the Use array when it is a prefix to the User object, and the number of Use objects is only known at allocation time
 CHungoffOperandTraitsHungoffOperandTraits - determine the allocation regime of the Use array when it is not a prefix to the User object, but allocated at an unrelated heap address

 COptPassGateExtensions to this class implement mechanisms to disable passes and individual optimizations at compile time
 COptBisectThis class implements a mechanism to disable passes and individual optimizations at compile time based on a command line option (-opt-bisect) in order to perform a bisecting search for optimization-related problems
 COptDisableThis class implements a mechanism to disable passes and individual optimizations at compile time based on a command line option (-opt-disable) in order to study how single transformations, or combinations thereof, affect the IR
 CPassInstrumentationCallbacksThis class manages callbacks registration, as well as provides a way for PassInstrumentation to pass control to the registered callbacks
 CPassInstrumentationThis class provides instrumentation entry points for the Pass Manager, doing calls to callbacks registered in PassInstrumentationCallbacks

 CX86TargetStreamerX86 target streamer implementing x86-only assembly directives
 CX86FPStackifierPassThis function returns a pass which converts floating-point register references and pseudo instructions into floating-point stack references and physical instructions
 CX86FixupLEAsPassReturn a pass that selectively replaces certain instructions (like add, sub, inc, dec, some shifts, and some multiplies) by equivalent LEA instructions, in order to eliminate execution delays in some processors
 CX86FixupInstTuningPassReturn a pass that replaces equivalent slower instructions with faster ones
 CX86FixupVectorConstantsPassReturn a pass that reduces the size of vector constant pool loads
 CX86OptimizeLEAsPassReturn a pass that removes redundant LEA instructions and redundant address recalculations
 CX86FixupSetCCPassReturn a pass that transforms setcc + movzx pairs into xor + setcc
 CX86AvoidStoreForwardingBlocksPassReturn a pass that avoids creating store forward block issues in the hardware
 CX86FlagsCopyLoweringPassReturn a pass that lowers EFLAGS copy pseudo instructions
 CX86DynAllocaExpanderPassReturn a pass that expands DynAlloca pseudo-instructions
 CX86TileConfigPassReturn a pass that config the tile registers
 CX86FastPreTileConfigPassReturn a pass that preconfig the tile registers before fast reg allocation
 CX86FastTileConfigPassReturn a pass that config the tile registers after fast reg allocation
 CX86PreTileConfigPassReturn a pass that insert pseudo tile config instruction
 CX86LowerTileCopyPassReturn a pass that lower the tile copy instruction
 CX86AvoidTrailingCallPassReturn a pass that inserts int3 at the end of the function if it ends with a CALL instruction
 CX86CallFrameOptimizationPassReturn a pass that optimizes the code-size of x86 call sequences
 CX86ExpandPseudoPassReturn a Machine IR pass that expands X86-specific pseudo instructions into a sequence of actual instructions
 CX86CmovConversionPassThis pass converts X86 cmov instructions into branch when profitable
 CX86FixupBWInstsPassReturn a Machine IR pass that selectively replaces certain byte and word instructions by equivalent 32 bit instructions, in order to eliminate partial register usage, false dependences on the upper portions of registers, and to save code size
 CX86DomainReassignmentPassReturn a Machine IR pass that reassigns instruction chains from one domain to another, when profitable
 CX86CompressEVEXPassThis pass compress instructions from EVEX space to legacy/VEX/EVEX space when possible in order to reduce code size or facilitate HW decoding
 CX86PartialReductionPassThis pass optimizes arithmetic based on knowledge that is only used by a reduction sequence and is therefore safe to reassociate in interesting ways
 CX86LowerAMXTypePassThe pass transforms load/store <256 x i32> to AMX load/store intrinsics or split the data to two <128 x i32>
 CX86SuppressAPXForRelocationPass
 CX86LowerAMXIntrinsicsPassThe pass transforms amx intrinsics to scalar operation if the function has optnone attribute or it is O0

 CInnerLoopVectorizerInnerLoopVectorizer vectorizes loops which contain only one basic block to a specified vectorization factor (VF)
 CEpilogueLoopVectorizationInfoEncapsulate information regarding vectorization of a loop and its epilogue
 CInnerLoopAndEpilogueVectorizerAn extension of the inner loop vectorizer that creates a skeleton for a vectorized loop that has its epilogue (residual) also vectorized
 CEpilogueVectorizerMainLoopA specialized derived class of inner loop vectorizer that performs vectorization of main loops in the process of vectorizing loops and their epilogues
 CEpilogueVectorizerEpilogueLoop
 CLoopVectorizationCostModelLoopVectorizationCostModel - estimates the expected speedups due to vectorization
 CCallWideningDecision
 CDenseMapInfo< BoUpSLP::EdgeInfo >
 CGraphTraits< BoUpSLP * >
 CChildIteratorTypeAdd the VectorizableTree to the index iterator to be able to return TreeEntry pointers
 Cnodes_iteratorFor the node iterator we just need to turn the TreeEntry iterator into a TreeEntry* iterator so that it dereferences to NodeRef
 CDOTGraphTraits< BoUpSLP * >
 CVPBlockBaseVPBlockBase is the building block of the Hierarchical Control-Flow Graph
 CVPRecipeBaseVPRecipeBase is a base class modeling a sequence of one or more output IR instructions
 CVPSingleDefRecipeVPSingleDef is a base class for recipes for modeling a sequence of one or more output IR that define a single result VPValue
 CVPIRFlagsClass to record and manage LLVM IR flags
 CWrapFlagsTy
 CTruncFlagsTy
 CDisjointFlagsTy
 CNonNegFlagsTy
 CVPRecipeWithIRFlagsA pure-virtual common base class for recipes defining a single VPValue and using IR flags
 CVPUnrollPartAccessorHelper to access the operand that contains the unroll part for this recipe after unrolling
 CVPIRMetadataHelper to manage IR metadata for recipes
 CVPInstructionThis is a concrete Recipe that models a single VPlan-level instruction
 CVPInstructionWithTypeA specialization of VPInstruction augmenting it with a dedicated result type, to be used when the opcode and operands of the VPInstruction don't directly determine the result type
 CVPPhiAccessorsHelper type to provide functions to access incoming values and blocks for phi-like recipes
 CVPPhi
 CVPIRInstructionA recipe to wrap on original IR instruction not to be modified during execution, except for PHIs
 CVPIRPhiAn overlay for VPIRInstructions wrapping PHI nodes enabling convenient use cast/dyn_cast/isa and execute() implementation
 CVPWidenRecipeVPWidenRecipe is a recipe for producing a widened instruction using the opcode and operands of the recipe
 CVPWidenCastRecipeVPWidenCastRecipe is a recipe to create vector cast instructions
 CVPWidenIntrinsicRecipeA recipe for widening vector intrinsics
 CVPWidenCallRecipeA recipe for widening Call instructions using library calls
 CVPHistogramRecipeA recipe representing a sequence of load -> update -> store as part of a histogram operation
 CVPWidenGEPRecipeA recipe for handling GEP instructions
 CVPVectorEndPointerRecipeA recipe to compute a pointer to the last element of each part of a widened memory access for widened memory accesses of IndexedTy
 CVPVectorPointerRecipeA recipe to compute the pointers for widened memory accesses of SourceElementTy
 CVPHeaderPHIRecipeA pure virtual base class for all recipes modeling header phis, including phis for first order recurrences, pointer inductions and reductions
 CVPWidenInductionRecipeBase class for widened induction (VPWidenIntOrFpInductionRecipe and VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe), providing shared functionality, including retrieving the step value, induction descriptor and original phi node
 CVPWidenIntOrFpInductionRecipeA recipe for handling phi nodes of integer and floating-point inductions, producing their vector values
 CVPWidenPointerInductionRecipe
 CVPWidenPHIRecipeA recipe for widened phis
 CVPFirstOrderRecurrencePHIRecipeA recipe for handling first-order recurrence phis
 CRdxOrderedPossible variants of a reduction
 CRdxInLoopThis reduction is in-loop
 CRdxUnorderedThis reduction is unordered with the partial result scaled down by some factor
 CVPReductionPHIRecipeA recipe for handling reduction phis
 CVPBlendRecipeA recipe for vectorizing a phi-node as a sequence of mask-based select instructions
 CVPInterleaveBaseA common base class for interleaved memory operations
 CVPInterleaveRecipeVPInterleaveRecipe is a recipe for transforming an interleave group of load or stores into one wide load/store and shuffles
 CVPInterleaveEVLRecipeA recipe for interleaved memory operations with vector-predication intrinsics
 CVPReductionRecipeA recipe to represent inloop, ordered or partial reduction operations
 CVPReductionEVLRecipeA recipe to represent inloop reduction operations with vector-predication intrinsics, performing a reduction on a vector operand with the explicit vector length (EVL) into a scalar value, and adding the result to a chain
 CVPReplicateRecipeVPReplicateRecipe replicates a given instruction producing multiple scalar copies of the original scalar type, one per lane, instead of producing a single copy of widened type for all lanes
 CVPBranchOnMaskRecipeA recipe for generating conditional branches on the bits of a mask
 CVPExpressionRecipeA recipe to combine multiple recipes into a single 'expression' recipe, which should be considered a single entity for cost-modeling and transforms
 CVPPredInstPHIRecipeVPPredInstPHIRecipe is a recipe for generating the phi nodes needed when control converges back from a Branch-on-Mask
 CVPWidenMemoryRecipeA common base class for widening memory operations
 CVPWidenLoadRecipeA recipe for widening load operations, using the address to load from and an optional mask
 CVPWidenLoadEVLRecipeA recipe for widening load operations with vector-predication intrinsics, using the address to load from, the explicit vector length and an optional mask
 CVPWidenStoreRecipeA recipe for widening store operations, using the stored value, the address to store to and an optional mask
 CVPWidenStoreEVLRecipeA recipe for widening store operations with vector-predication intrinsics, using the value to store, the address to store to, the explicit vector length and an optional mask
 CVPExpandSCEVRecipeRecipe to expand a SCEV expression
 CVPCanonicalIVPHIRecipeCanonical scalar induction phi of the vector loop
 CVPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipeA recipe for generating the active lane mask for the vector loop that is used to predicate the vector operations
 CVPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipeA recipe for generating the phi node for the current index of elements, adjusted in accordance with EVL value
 CVPWidenCanonicalIVRecipeA Recipe for widening the canonical induction variable of the vector loop
 CVPDerivedIVRecipeA recipe for converting the input value IV value to the corresponding value of an IV with different start and step values, using Start + IV * Step
 CVPScalarIVStepsRecipeA recipe for handling phi nodes of integer and floating-point inductions, producing their scalar values
 CCastIsPossible< VPPhiAccessors, const VPRecipeBase * >Casting from VPRecipeBase -> VPPhiAccessors is supported for all recipe types implementing VPPhiAccessors
 CCastInfoVPPhiAccessorsSupport casting from VPRecipeBase -> VPPhiAccessors, by down-casting to the recipe types implementing VPPhiAccessors
 CCastInfo< VPPhiAccessors, VPRecipeBase * >
 CCastInfo< VPPhiAccessors, const VPRecipeBase * >
 CCastInfoVPIRMetadataSupport casting from VPRecipeBase -> VPIRMetadata, by down-casting to the recipe types implementing VPIRMetadata
 CCastInfo< VPIRMetadata, VPRecipeBase * >
 CCastInfo< VPIRMetadata, const VPRecipeBase * >
 CVPBasicBlockVPBasicBlock serves as the leaf of the Hierarchical Control-Flow Graph
 CVPIRBasicBlockA special type of VPBasicBlock that wraps an existing IR basic block
 CVPRegionBlockVPRegionBlock represents a collection of VPBasicBlocks and VPRegionBlocks which form a Single-Entry-Single-Exiting subgraph of the output IR CFG
 CVPlanVPlan models a candidate for vectorization, encoding various decisions take to produce efficient output IR, including which branches, basic-blocks and output IR instructions to generate, and their cost
 CVPTypeAnalysisAn analysis for type-inference for VPValues
 CVPRegisterUsageA struct that represents some properties of the register usage of a loop
 CVPAllSuccessorsIteratorIterator to traverse all successors of a VPBlockBase node
 CVPBlockDeepTraversalWrapperHelper for GraphTraits specialization that traverses through VPRegionBlocks
 CGraphTraits< VPBlockDeepTraversalWrapper< VPBlockBase * > >GraphTraits specialization to recursively traverse VPBlockBase nodes, including traversing through VPRegionBlocks
 CGraphTraits< VPBlockDeepTraversalWrapper< const VPBlockBase * > >
 CVPBlockShallowTraversalWrapperHelper for GraphTraits specialization that does not traverses through VPRegionBlocks

 CVFRangeA range of powers-of-2 vectorization factors with fixed start and adjustable end
 CiteratorIterator to iterate over vectorization factors in a VFRange
 CVPLaneIn what follows, the term "input IR" refers to code that is fed into the vectorizer whereas the term "output IR" refers to code that is generated by the vectorizer
 CVPTransformStateVPTransformState holds information passed down when "executing" a VPlan, needed for generating the output IR
 CDataState
 CCFGStateHold state information used when constructing the CFG of the output IR, traversing the VPBasicBlocks and generating corresponding IR BasicBlocks
 CVPCostContextStruct to hold various analysis needed for cost computations
 CVPSlotTrackerThis class can be used to assign names to VPValues
 CVPlanPrinterVPlanPrinter prints a given VPlan to a given output stream
 CVPInterleavedAccessInfo
 CVPlanSlpClass that maps (parts of) an existing VPlan to trees of combined VPInstructions
 CVPlanTransforms
 CVPBlockUtilsClass that provides utilities for VPBlockBases in VPlan
 CVPValueThis is the base class of the VPlan Def/Use graph, used for modeling the data flow into, within and out of the VPlan
 CVPIRValueA VPValue representing a live-in from the input IR or a constant
 CVPSymbolicValueA symbolic live-in VPValue, used for values like vector trip count, VF, and VFxUF
 CVPRecipeValueA VPValue defined by a recipe that produces one or more values
 CVPUserThis class augments VPValue with operands which provide the inverse def-use edges from VPValue's users to their defs
 CVPDefThis class augments a recipe with a set of VPValues defined by the recipe
 CPartialReductionChainA chain of instructions that form a partial reduction
